target.title: fix(codex): keep root memory in bootstrap context
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94295.

Native Codex currently suppresses root workspace `MEMORY.md` when memory tools are available, causing `/context` to show the file as present but injected with `0` chars. That makes sparse bootstrap memory unavailable unless the model already knows to ask memory tools for the right fact.

This PR keeps root `MEMORY.md` in the bounded workspace bootstrap prompt context and leaves memory tools available for deeper recall.

## Changes

- Stop selecting root `MEMORY.md` as a tool-routed bootstrap file for native Codex turns.
- Stop filtering root `MEMORY.md` out of `buildBootstrapContextForFiles(...)` when memory tools are present.
- Stop excluding root memory from Codex prompt-context selection.
- Update Codex app-server tests to expect nonzero `MEMORY.md` injection unless normal bootstrap budget truncation applies.
- Update the Codex harness reference, Codex harness runtime, token-use, and system-prompt docs for the new native Codex `MEMORY.md` default.

## Why

`MEMORY.md` is the sparse long-term bootstrap memory surface. It can contain ambient operating facts that the model needs before it can know which memory query to run. Tool-based recall is useful, but it should not replace bootstrap memory by default.

Related broader routing work: #83612. This PR is intentionally narrower and addresses the direct `MEMORY.md injected 0 chars` regression.

## Validation

Focused local validation on the Raspberry Pi checkout at `385b6f50a17a62555ce1abd30c28c9c9b8fa2978`:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex-app-server-attempt-support.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex-app-server-attempt.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "keeps MEMORY.md in bounded context"
git diff --check
rg -n 'not pasted into every native Codex|not pasted into native Codex|do not paste raw `MEMORY\.md`|workspace-memory note|read on demand through memory tools|tool-routed `MEMORY\.md`|MEMORY\.md` pointers|fallback injection' docs/concepts/system-prompt.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md docs/reference/token-use.md || true
```

Results:

- `attempt-context.test.ts`: 4 passed.
- Focused `run-attempt.test.ts` memory case: 1 passed, 104 skipped by `-t`.
- `git diff --check`: no output.
- Stale-doc wording scan: no output.

## Real behavior proof

**After-fix real behavior proof from Nick's Raspberry Pi OpenClaw host:**

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Native Codex was suppressing root workspace `MEMORY.md` when memory tools were available, causing `/context` to show the file as present but injected with `0` chars. The fix keeps root `MEMORY.md` in the bounded workspace bootstrap prompt context while retaining memory tools for deeper recall.
- **Real environment tested:** Nick's real Raspberry Pi 5 OpenClaw host, running the installed OpenClaw package reported as `OpenClaw 2026.6.6 (8c802aa)`. The installed runtime bundle patched in-place was `/home/rpiuser_nick/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
openclaw --version
rg -n "MEMORY.md is injected|OpenClaw does not paste|memoryToolRoutedBootstrapFiles = \\[\\]|excludeMemory: false" \
  /home/rpiuser_nick/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js \
  /home/rpiuser_nick/projects/active/openclaw-pr81185-rebase/extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts
node --check /home/rpiuser_nick/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js
openclaw config validate
git -C /home/rpiuser_nick/projects/active/openclaw-pr81185-rebase diff --check -- \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Actual terminal output from the real OpenClaw host after patching:

```text
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.6 (8c802aa)

$ rg -n "MEMORY.md is injected|OpenClaw does not paste|memoryToolRoutedBootstrapFiles = \\[\\]|excludeMemory: false" \
  /home/rpiuser_nick/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js \
  /home/rpiuser_nick/projects/active/openclaw-pr81185-rebase/extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts
/home/rpiuser_nick/projects/active/openclaw-pr81185-rebase/extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts:207:      excludeMemory: false,
/home/rpiuser_nick/projects/active/openclaw-pr81185-rebase/extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts:762:    `MEMORY.md is injected through the bounded workspace context when present. Use ${toolNames.join(" or ")} for deeper durable memory beyond the injected bootstrap context.`,
/home/rpiuser_nick/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js:274:        const memoryToolRoutedBootstrapFiles = [];
/home/rpiuser_nick/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js:286:          excludeMemory: false,
/home/rpiuser_nick/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js:630:        `MEMORY.md is injected through the bounded workspace context when present. Use ${(params.toolNames?.length ? params.toolNames : Array.from(CODEX_MEMORY_TOOL_NAMES)).join(" or ")} for deeper durable memory beyond the injected bootstrap context.`,

$ node --check /home/rpiuser_nick/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/run-attempt-BtRhrKv-.js
# no output, exit 0

$ openclaw config validate
Config valid: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json

$ git -C /home/rpiuser_nick/projects/active/openclaw-pr81185-rebase diff --check -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
# no output, exit 0
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The installed native Codex runtime path no longer contains the suppressing `OpenClaw does not paste` behavior, no longer filters root `MEMORY.md` out of prompt context when memory tools are available, and now routes `MEMORY.md` through normal bounded bootstrap context (`excludeMemory: false`) while keeping memory tools available for deeper recall.


- **What was not tested:** A restarted-production-Gateway live `/context` screenshot or transcript was not captured in this PR body because Gateway restarts are held behind the normal operator approval gate. The after-fix evidence above covers the patched native Codex runtime path and validation commands on the real Pi host.

Hosted proof gate previously passed on the updated PR with: `External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.`

## Maintainer note

This intentionally changes the native Codex prompt-memory default: root `MEMORY.md` is now automatically model-visible through bounded workspace context when present, even when memory tools are available. Memory tools remain the path for deeper durable recall beyond sparse bootstrap facts.

## Not yet captured

A redacted live `/context` screenshot/output from a restarted production Gateway has not been captured here. Restarting the production Gateway is held behind the normal operator approval gate.

target.title: fix(tools): surface empty availability group diagnostics at descriptor registration
target.body:
> **AI-assisted PR.** Implemented and verified with Claude (Claude Code). I have reviewed and understand the change and own the final diff.

## Summary

- Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups silently hid tools from the plan with no observable signal. Plugin authors had no way to know their descriptor was malformed.
- Root cause: `capturePluginToolDescriptor` did not preserve tool-authored `availability` expressions, and no validation ran at registration time.
- Fix: preserve `availability` on captured descriptors and surface `unsupported-signal` diagnostics via `console.warn` once at descriptor-registration time (not in the planner hot path).

Fixes #92361

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**:
Malformed tool descriptors with empty availability groups now produce a visible `console.warn` at descriptor-registration time instead of silently hiding the tool with no signal.

**Real environment tested**:
Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node.js v24.13.1, OpenClaw PR branch @ d8907360, tsx for runtime probing.

**Before-fix evidence**:
```text
# Empty anyOf → tool silently hidden, no warning, no log
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { capturePluginToolDescriptor } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.js';
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'proof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { anyOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});
console.log('No warning was emitted — tool silently captured with broken availability');
"
# Output: No warning was emitted — tool silently captured with broken availability
```

The tool descriptor is captured and later hidden by `buildToolPlan` with zero observable signal. Plugin authors have no way to diagnose why their tool never appears in the plan.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { capturePluginToolDescriptor } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.js';

// Test 1: empty anyOf
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'proof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { anyOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});

// Test 2: empty allOf
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'allof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { allOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});

// Test 3: valid expression (no warning expected)
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'valid_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { kind: 'always' } } as any,
  optional: false,
});
"
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```text
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/proof_tool): Empty availability anyOf group
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/allof_tool): Empty availability allOf group
```

- Empty `anyOf` → `console.warn` emitted with pluginId + tool name + diagnostic message ✅
- Empty `allOf` → `console.warn` emitted with pluginId + tool name + diagnostic message ✅
- Valid `kind: "always"` → no warning (correctly silent) ✅
- No availability → no warning (backward compatible) ✅

**Observed result after the fix**:
Descriptor authors immediately see which tool has a malformed availability expression at registration time (once per captured descriptor — descriptors are cached). The evaluator and planner stay pure.

**What was not tested**:
Live gateway startup with a real external plugin that has malformed availability metadata. The descriptor capture path operates on in-memory descriptors and has no platform-specific logic.

## Tests and validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       10 passed (10)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       67 passed (67)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       6 passed (6)

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false
# passed
```

New test cases:
1. warns when tool has empty availability `anyOf` group
2. warns when tool has empty availability `allOf` group
3. does not warn for valid availability expression
4. does not warn when tool has no availability
5. does not warn for non-expression availability metadata
6. preserves availability on the captured descriptor

## Risk / scope

- User-visible behavior change: No — descriptors are a developer contract, not user-facing runtime behavior
- Config/environment/migration: No
- Security/auth/network: No
- Highest risk: `console.warn` fires once per captured descriptor at registration time. Descriptors are cached after first capture, so the warning fires exactly once per unique tool per cache lifetime.
- Mitigation: `unsupported-signal` only fires for malformed descriptors (empty allOf/anyOf, unknown expression shapes). Runtime conditions like auth-missing are explicitly excluded.

## What did NOT change (scope boundary)

- `evaluateToolAvailability` / `evaluateExpression` — still pure, no side effects
- `buildToolPlan` — still a deterministic planner, no callbacks
- `AnyAgentTool` type — no new contract field; reading through narrowed record (same pattern as existing `label` read)
- Plugin SDK exports — no public API surface change

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: plugin-sdk: add runtime.session.cancel and channel.outbound.sendToSession
target.body:
## Summary

- add `runtime.session.cancel()` to the native plugin runtime with graceful `not-wired` fallback
- add `runtime.channel.outbound.sendToSession()` for direct text delivery to a session’s last recorded route
- wire gateway plugin runtimes to existing ACP `cancelSession` path and update plugin runtime mocks/API baseline

## Motivation

Phase C P1 needs a safe Plugin SDK route for latency-preemption plugins such as `gandalf-preempt` to cancel an in-flight session turn and post a G1/preemption signal without reaching into internal gateway objects or bot-token state.

## Behavior

- Additive Plugin SDK surface only; no behavior change for existing callers.
- `runtime.session.cancel({ sessionKey, reason })` returns `{ cancelled: false, reason: "not-wired" }` when no gateway binding is available.
- `runtime.channel.outbound.sendToSession({ sessionKey, text, identity })` resolves `lastChannel`, `lastTo`, `lastAccountId`, and `lastThreadId` from the session store, then sends through the existing outbound adapter pipeline.
- `sendToSession` returns `{ delivered: false, error: "no-route-found" }` if no session route is available.

## Real behavior proof

After-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup on `golfsimserver`:

```console
$ openclaw status --json
{
  "runtimeVersion": "2026.5.7",
  "heartbeat": {
    "defaultAgentId": "main",
    "agents": [
      { "agentId": "main", "enabled": true, "every": "2h", "everyMs": 7200000 },
      { "agentId": "aragorn", "enabled": true, "every": "2h", "everyMs": 7200000 },
      { "agentId": "samwise", "enabled": true, "every": "2h", "everyMs": 7200000 }
    ]
  },
  "sessions": {
    "count": 90,
    "defaults": { "model": "claude-opus-4-7", "contextTokens": 200000 },
    "recent": [
      {
        "agentId": "main",
        "key": "agent:main:direct:1498056540551118959",
        "kind": "direct",
        "model": "gpt-5.5",
        "runtime": "OpenClaw Pi Default"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

This confirms the target host has live OpenClaw runtime state and recorded session routes, which is the runtime data source used by `sendToSession()`.

## Testing

- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy
target.body:
## Summary

New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns `bun wrapper.ts` which boots a loopback HTTPS MITM proxy on 127.0.0.1, taps every Anthropic API SSE event, and re-emits them as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records. This lets Claude run in interactive (subscription) mode while preserving the streaming surface that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on.

**Why:** Starting June 15, 2026, `claude -p` (headless/programmatic) mode draws from a separate fixed monthly credit pool billed at API rates. The interactive backend avoids this by keeping Claude in subscription mode.

## Scope boundary

- Existing `claude-cli` backend **untouched** — shared gate functions extended via `isClaudeCliCompatibleBackend(provider)` to recognise both backend IDs
- User config overrides inherit via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped
- System CA store not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to spawned child only)
- Non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified

## Key files

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| Wrapper + MITM proxy | `extensions/anthropic/src/interactive-proxy/wrapper.ts`, `mitm-server.ts`, `cert-manager.ts`, `tty-spoof.cjs` |
| Backend config | `extensions/anthropic/src/cli-backend-interactive.ts`, `normalizeConfig` |
| Shared gates | `src/agents/cli-backends.ts`, `provider-id.ts`, `model-runtime-aliases.ts` |
| Execution wiring | `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` (reasoning bridge dedup) |

## Security notes

- Per-turn local TLS MITM on loopback only; system trust store untouched
- CA + leaf key cached under `~/.openclaw/proxy-certs/` at 0700/0600
- Auth tokens forwarded byte-for-byte, never read/logged by wrapper
- Spilled-prompt overflow files in per-run tempdir (0700), per-file 0600

## Test plan

- [x] `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` — 5 cases covering `normalizeConfig` branches
- [x] Live tested: Windows 11, Node v24, Opus 4.6/4.8 over Telegram
- [ ] Linux/macOS (developed on Windows only)

## Live proof (2026-05-29, current head)

**Gateway startup:**
```
2026-05-29T20:49:39 agent model: claude-cli-interactive/claude-opus-4-8 (thinking=max, fast=off)
2026-05-29T20:49:39 http server listening (6 plugins; 18.4s)
2026-05-29T20:49:40 gateway ready
```

**Turn dispatch (interactive proxy + session resume):**
```
2026-05-29T21:04:17 cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=claude-opus-4-6 trigger=user useResume=true session=present reuse=reusable
2026-05-29T21:04:17 cli argv: bun .../interactive-proxy/wrapper.ts --resume 23c8a0d6-... --model opus
```

**Telegram — interactive proxy streaming reasoning + tool events:**

![proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/2ec1bed2aa0caf2a0a8311a458dcd1bc532ce68c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-29.jpg)

target.title: docs(browser): resolve networkidle contradiction across browser docs
target.body:
## Summary

- `docs/tools/browser-control.md` advertises `openclaw browser wait --load networkidle` without any profile qualifier, while `docs/tools/browser.md` and `docs/cli/browser.md` say it is not supported — with no qualifier on the "not supported" side to indicate the restriction is profile-scoped. Reported in #80587.
- Qualifies all three docs pages to state that `networkidle` works on managed `openclaw` and raw/remote CDP profiles, but not on `existing-session`/`user` profiles. The runtime already enforces this boundary (`existing-session-limits.ts:29` rejects `networkidle` for existing-session waits; `pw-tools-core.interactions.ts:1240` supports it on Playwright-backed profiles).

## Linked context

Closes #80587

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Docs contradiction on `browser wait --load networkidle` support.
- Real environment tested: Local checkout on `upstream/main` (8142c12db2).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```text
rg -n 'networkidle' docs/tools/browser-control.md docs/tools/browser.md docs/cli/browser.md
```
- Evidence after fix:
```text
docs/cli/browser.md:318:- `wait --load networkidle` is not supported on existing-session profiles (works on managed and raw/remote CDP)
docs/tools/browser.md:746:- **Wait / upload / dialog** - `wait --url` supports exact, substring, and glob patterns; `wait --load networkidle` is not supported on existing-session profiles (it works on managed and raw/remote CDP profiles). [...]
docs/tools/browser-control.md:324:  - `openclaw browser wait --load networkidle`
docs/tools/browser-control.md:325:  - Supported on managed `openclaw` and raw/remote CDP profiles. The `user` and `existing-session` profiles reject `networkidle`; use `--url`, `--text`, a selector, or `--fn` waits there.
```
  All three pages now carry a profile-scoped qualifier; no unqualified "is not supported" or unqualified positive example remains.
- Observed result after fix: Contradiction resolved — each page states the exact profile boundary.
- What was not tested: No live browser session was needed; this is a docs-only change verified by reading the patched Markdown.
- Proof limitations: Docs accuracy depends on the runtime boundary staying unchanged; I confirmed the runtime guard (`existing-session-limits.ts:29`, `agent.act.ts:349`) and Playwright wait path (`pw-tools-core.interactions.ts:1240`) still enforce the same split on current `upstream/main`.

## Tests and validation

- `git diff --check` — clean (no whitespace errors)
- Diff is 3 files, +3/-2 lines — no runtime, config, or code changes

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No` (docs-only)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: Docs accuracy if the runtime boundary changes in a future release

How is that risk mitigated? The profile-scoped wording matches the current runtime contract; a future runtime change would naturally require a docs update anyway.

Current review state: maintainer review and CI.

AI-assisted (Claude Code). Proof above was verified by manual terminal grep on patched files.

target.title: fix(plugins): enhance plugin API compatibility diagnostic messages with troubleshooting guidance
target.body:
## Summary

Plugin API compatibility discovery-skip warnings now include actionable troubleshooting guidance so users can self-diagnose version mismatches instead of seeing a bare error.

- **Problem**: When plugin discovery is skipped due to plugin API version mismatch (e.g., "host is 2026.5.28" on a 2026.6.8 install), the warning message provides no guidance on how to diagnose or resolve the mismatch.
- **Solution**: Extend the two `shouldSkipIncompatiblePackagePluginApi` diagnostic messages with hints pointing to `openclaw --version`, `OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION`, and `openclaw doctor`.
- **What changed**: `src/plugins/discovery.ts` — 2 diagnostic messages enhanced (2 lines)
- **What did NOT change**: No runtime behavior, no version resolution, no discovery logic, no config schema, no other plugin metadata paths

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #93908
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Issue #93908 reports a Feishu plugin discovery failure where the gateway skips the plugin with the message "plugin requires plugin API >=2026.6.8, but this host is 2026.5.28; skipping discovery" despite a 2026.6.8 install. The reporter has no idea what to check next — the message states the problem but offers no path to resolution. Meanwhile, the install path (`src/plugins/install.ts:207-213`) already includes troubleshooting hints like "Upgrade OpenClaw and retry" and "set OPENCLAW_VERSION and retry." This PR brings the discovery-skip diagnostics to the same standard.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Plugin discovery-skip warnings for API version mismatches and invalid metadata now include actionable troubleshooting guidance.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux (x64), Node v22, branch `fix/feishu-plugin-api-diag-93908` at commit `5a52bf10f0`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# 1. Run existing discovery tests (messageIncludes assertions)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/discovery.test.ts --run

# 2. Verify the diagnostic message strings in the source
git diff origin/main...HEAD

# 3. Typecheck the changed file
pnpm tsgo
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/discovery.test.ts --run
 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668000999/OpenClaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  76 passed (76)
   Start at  16:03:52
   Duration  5.97s

$ git diff origin/main...HEAD
diff --git a/src/plugins/discovery.ts b/src/plugins/discovery.ts
index 546d77ac03..185cf3fa97 100644
--- a/src/plugins/discovery.ts
+++ b/src/plugins/discovery.ts
@@ -891,7 +891,7 @@ function shouldSkipIncompatiblePackagePluginApi(params: {
     params.diagnostics.push({
       level: "warn",
       source: path.join(params.packageDir, "package.json"),
-      message: `invalid package plugin API metadata: ${packagePluginApiRangeCheck.error}; skipping discovery`,
+      message: `invalid package plugin API metadata: ${packagePluginApiRangeCheck.error}; skipping discovery (check package.json openclaw.compat.pluginApi)`,
       ...(pluginId ? { pluginId } : {}),
     });
     return true;
@@ -910,7 +910,7 @@ function shouldSkipIncompatiblePackagePluginApi(params: {
   params.diagnostics.push({
     level: "warn",
     source: path.join(params.packageDir, "package.json"),
-    message: `plugin requires plugin API ${packagePluginApiRange}, but this host is ${compatibilityHostVersion}; skipping discovery`,
+    message: `plugin requires plugin API ${packagePluginApiRange}, but this host is ${compatibilityHostVersion}; skipping discovery (check "openclaw --version", OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION, or run "openclaw doctor")`,
     ...(pluginId ? { pluginId } : {}),
   });
   return true;
```

**Before/After comparison**:

| Diagnostic | Before | After |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Invalid metadata | `invalid package plugin API metadata: …; skipping discovery` | `… (check package.json openclaw.compat.pluginApi)` |
| Version mismatch | `plugin requires plugin API >=X, but this host is Y; skipping discovery` | `… (check "openclaw --version", OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION, or run "openclaw doctor")` |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. All 76 discovery tests pass with `messageIncludes` matching — the added text does not break existing assertions
  2. The invalid-metadata diagnostic now points users to the specific `package.json` field to check
  3. The version-mismatch diagnostic now provides 3 actionable next steps: verify gateway version, check env vars, run doctor
  4. Message format aligns with the existing pattern in `src/plugins/install.ts:207-213` which already includes troubleshooting guidance
- **What was not tested**:
  1. Live Windows npm-global install with Feishu plugin (requires Windows environment)
  2. Actual `openclaw doctor` flow end-to-end with stale version metadata
  3. Gateway startup with `OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION` override on Windows

## Root Cause

N/A — this is a diagnostic UX improvement. The underlying version mismatch in #93908 is most likely environmental (stale npm install, PATH issues, or `OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION` / `npm_package_version` env-var leakage). The message now equips users to self-diagnose.

## Regression Test Plan

N/A — existing `src/plugins/discovery.test.ts` tests (76 passing) already cover the message content via `messageIncludes` assertions. The enhanced messages are backward-compatible with these assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who encounter a plugin API compatibility discovery-skip will now see diagnostic messages with troubleshooting hints:

- `… skipping discovery (check package.json openclaw.compat.pluginApi)`
- `… skipping discovery (check "openclaw --version", OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION, or run "openclaw doctor")`

No other user-visible changes.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (x64)
- Runtime/container: Node v22
- Branch: `fix/feishu-plugin-api-diag-93908`
- Commit: `5a52bf10f0`

### Steps

1. `git checkout fix/feishu-plugin-api-diag-93908`
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/discovery.test.ts --run`
3. Verify all 76 tests pass

### Expected

All existing `messageIncludes` assertions pass; enhanced messages include troubleshooting hints.

### Actual

76/76 tests pass, diff shows 2 diagnostic messages enhanced with parenthetical guidance.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Diagnostic message strings in source diff; all 76 discovery tests pass
- **Edge cases checked**: `messageIncludes` assertion compatibility — adding text does not break substring matching
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live gateway startup with Feishu plugin on Windows; actual user seeing the new message on a failing install

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — only changes warning text, no behavioral change
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — targeted UX improvement at the exact point where users hit the diagnostic gap
- **Refactor needed**: No — the `shouldSkipIncompatiblePackagePluginApi` function already owns the right boundary
- **Alternative considered**: Updating `devDependencies.openclaw` across 30 extensions from `2026.5.28` to `2026.6.8`. Rejected because devDependencies do not affect runtime version resolution; fixing the diagnostic message directly addresses the user pain point with minimal blast radius.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude (DeepSeek v4 Pro) <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: None. This change only extends warning message strings; it does not alter discovery logic, version resolution, or any runtime behavior.

**Mitigation**: 76 existing tests confirm `messageIncludes` compatibility. No behavioral regression possible.

---

Related to #93908

target.title: feat(tools/write): add append mode for agent writes
target.body:
## Summary

Revives and rebases the closed append-mode work from #75549 for the canonical data-loss issue #40001.

The `write` tool currently overwrites existing files. OpenClaw has an append-only wrapper for memory-flush runs, but ordinary agent turns, cron skills, and monitor-and-react workflows do not have a first-class append path. This PR adds a backwards-compatible optional `append: true` parameter to `write` so agents can append without synthesizing a read-concatenate-overwrite sequence.

When `append` is omitted or false, existing overwrite behavior is unchanged.

## What changed

- Adds optional `append?: boolean` handling for host/workspace and sandbox write operations.
- Preserves existing workspace/root guard behavior.
- Serializes concurrent appends through the existing file mutation queue.
- Preserves validation behavior for blank paths and invalid content.
- Surfaces sandbox I/O failures except for missing-file cases used to create-on-append.
- Honors aborted append calls before entering the mutation queue.
- Adds focused regression coverage for append-create, append-accumulate, append-false-overwrites, validation, and sandbox/workspace behavior.

## Validation

Run in an isolated checkout on current `origin/main`:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.params.test.ts`
  - passed: `10 files / 170 tests`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm lint:core`
  - passed: `0 warnings / 0 errors`

## Notes

This PR intentionally keeps the API small and backwards-compatible. It does not remove or relax the existing memory-flush append-only wrapper; it gives ordinary `write` callers an explicit append path instead of relying on prompt discipline or destructive read/concatenate/write patterns.

Fixes #40001.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The agent write tool supports `append: true` without overwriting the existing file, while still using the workspace-root guard for workspace-only host writes.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux using the actual `createHostWorkspaceWriteTool` implementation against a temporary workspace on the real filesystem.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx` terminal smoke that created the host workspace write tool, wrote `first line`, reran the same write tool with `append: true` for `second line`, then read the resulting file.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the smoke:

  ```text
  workspace: $TMPDIR/openclaw-write-append-proof-.../workspace
  tool result: append:true preserved existing content and added the second line
  file content:
  first line
  second line
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The final file retained the original content and appended the new content in order; the append path did not truncate or replace the file.
- **What was not tested:** No live Gateway session was exercised; validation used the real tool implementation in an isolated local checkout plus targeted regression/type checks.

target.title: fix(discord): guard message-tool-only final delivery
target.body:
## Summary

Discord `message_tool_only` turns can currently end without any visible source-channel delivery when the model does not call the `message` tool:

- terminal `NO_REPLY` despite an owed visible status/result;
- empty assistant final after tool work;
- substantive normal final text that is then suppressed by message-tool-only source reply policy.

Prompt/tool-description guidance helps, but it is advisory. This adds a narrow runtime guard at the `runReplyAgent` result/suppression boundary.

## What changed

- Detect Discord `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` turns with no committed successful message-tool/media/target delivery evidence.
- For substantive final payloads, mark them with existing `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` metadata so dispatch can surface them instead of silently suppressing them.
- For `NO_REPLY` / empty-final paths, synthesize a visible guard/error payload, also marked for source delivery.
- For non-mentioned Discord threads/channels where silent replies are otherwise allowed, keep pure ambient/no-tool `NO_REPLY` quiet but treat tool activity, block progress, cron side effects, or substantive final text as response-owed evidence.
- Also cover Discord tool/progress activity followed by empty/`NO_REPLY` finals when `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` is not `message_tool_only`; real thread traces showed that path can occur while visible progress/tool chatter is emitted but no final/status answer is delivered.
- Keep privacy/silence boundaries:
  - skip heartbeats;
  - skip `room_event` strict/background turns;
  - skip turns with committed message-tool delivery evidence;
  - keep unaddressed group/channel silence quiet when empty replies are allowed.

## Related context

This complements, rather than replaces, nearby work:

- #84022 — current Discord transcript-only/suppressed final class.
- #84328 — nearby normal-text fallback when the model skips the message tool (closed unmerged at PR preparation time).
- #84232 — best-effort for implicit tool-only source replies / `reconcileUnknownSend` path (open at PR preparation time).
- #82609 — merged strict `message_tool` policy baseline.
- #80949 — earlier diagnostic/recovery approach (closed unmerged); this PR uses a targeted runtime delivery guard instead.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Discord `message_tool_only` turns should no longer end with no visible source-channel reply when a directly addressed turn produces `NO_REPLY`, an empty final, or normal final text without a successful `message.send`.
- **Real setup tested**: Real OpenClaw deployment on Linux/arm64, installed package `OpenClaw 2026.5.12 (f066dd2)`, with the same runtime-guard logic from this PR ported to the installed compiled runtime. The deployment was restarted after applying the patch so the running gateway loaded the updated runtime.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Applied the PR-shaped runtime guard to the installed runtime bundle.
  2. Restarted the real gateway process.
  3. Ran the updated hotfix verifier and syntax check against the installed runtime bundle after the panel-reviewed visible-reply-evidence revision.
  4. Captured a follow-up real deployment regression for non-mentioned Discord thread/channel tool work followed by `NO_REPLY` / empty final, then ported the response-owed guard to source and to the installed runtime bundle on disk.
  5. Ran the response-owed hotfix verifier and `node --check` against the installed runtime bundle.
  6. Confirmed the gateway process start time was after the earlier visible-evidence patch was applied; the response-owed disk hotfix still requires a later gateway restart to become active in the running gateway.
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal output from the real deployment:

```text
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.12 (f066dd2)

$ ps -eo pid,lstart,cmd | grep '[n]ode .*openclaw.*gateway'
<PID> Wed May 20 07:36:08 2026 /usr/bin/node <openclaw-install>/dist/index.js gateway --port <gateway-port>

$ node <proof-script> --verify
{
  "ok": true,
  "file": "<openclaw-install>/dist/agent-runner.runtime-<hash>.js",
  "verification": {
    "version": "2026.5.12",
    "prShapeSentinel": true,
    "visibleEvidenceSentinel": true,
    "responseOwedSentinel": true,
    "marker": true,
    "importedGetReplyPayloadMetadata": true,
    "softerGuardText": true,
    "selectiveHelper": true,
    "skipsBlockedPayloads": true,
    "preservesAlreadyMarkedReentry": true,
    "noBroadMap": true,
    "guardUsesVisibleEvidence": true,
    "ignoresToolProgressEvidence": true,
    "excludesCronAddsFromGuardEvidence": true,
    "traceSynthesized": true,
    "traceMarking": true,
    "drainComment": true,
    "finalMark": true,
    "substantiveFinalHelper": true,
    "predicateUsesResponseActivity": true,
    "responseActivityFromTools": true,
    "responseActivityFromBlockProgress": true,
    "responseActivityFromCron": true,
    "ok": true
  }
}

$ node --check <openclaw-install>/dist/agent-runner.runtime-<hash>.js
exit status: 0
```

- **Observed result after fix**: After the earlier restart, the real deployment was running with the PR-shaped runtime guard loaded. The verifier confirmed the guard text, selective first-substantive-payload marking, `beforeAgentRunBlocked` skip, re-entry preservation for already-marked payloads, guard trace logging, pending-tool-drain ordering comment, and the narrower visible-reply evidence split. A follow-up response-owed disk hotfix verifier confirmed the non-mentioned Discord thread/channel coverage. A later real trace showed an additional gap where `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` was not `message_tool_only` and `toolSummary` was absent even though visible tool/progress chatter existed; the latest source update adds a regression test for that path and treats successful progress/side-effect activity as response-owed activity while still keeping pure ambient/no-tool silence quiet.
- **What was not tested**: I did not run the upstream PR branch itself as the production gateway. The source PR head is covered by the targeted tests above; the real-environment proof covers the equivalent compiled runtime seam after applying the same guard logic to an installed deployment. The deployment proof is used as real-behavior evidence for the guard seam; source tests cover the latest refined predicate. Local installed-runtime hotfixes are temporary validation artifacts and may require a separate restart to match the latest source head exactly.


### Latest v3 refinement

After applying the response-owed variant in a real deployment, follow-up Discord traces exposed an additional path: the source reply mode was not `message_tool_only`, but the run still emitted tool/progress chatter and then ended with an empty final. The latest source head handles that path while avoiding a false positive where block-streamed content already delivered a substantive automatic reply:

- Discord + no committed visible final evidence + tool/progress activity + empty/`NO_REPLY` final ⇒ guard payload.
- Non-`message_tool_only` substantive final payloads stay on the normal automatic-delivery path.
- `message_tool_only` substantive finals are still marked for source delivery.
- Room events, explicit silent-expected turns, committed `message.send` evidence, and approval prompts still skip the guard.

## Validation

Validated in an isolated disposable checkout; no live Gateway/runtime/config/state was used.

```text
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
1 test file passed; 67 tests passed.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Typecheck for the core test tsconfig passed.
```

target.title: fix(agent): resolve compaction model alias to canonical model ref
target.body:
## Summary

- Auto-compaction dispatched a bare model alias (e.g. `gpt54mini`) as-is instead of resolving it to its canonical model reference, causing `Unknown model: openai/gpt54mini` errors
- Resolve bare `agents.defaults.compaction.model` overrides through the shared model-selection helpers: literal configured model ids win before alias lookup, then `buildModelAliasIndex` + `resolveModelRefFromString` for alias matches
- When the alias resolves to a different provider, the auth profile is correctly dropped (same behavior as explicit `provider/model` overrides)
- When no alias matches, the override is used literally with the session provider (backward-compatible fallback)

Ref #90340

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Auto-compaction model alias resolution. A bare alias in `agents.defaults.compaction.model` (e.g. `gpt54mini`) is now resolved to its canonical model ref (e.g. `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`) before dispatch, while configured literal model ids keep precedence over alias collisions.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, production function via `node --import tsx`.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-runtime-context.ts';
const cfg = { agents: { defaults: { models: { 'openai/gpt-5.4-mini': { alias: 'gpt54mini' }, 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-6': { alias: 'thinky' } } } } };
const r1 = resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget({ config: { ...cfg, agents: { defaults: { ...cfg.agents.defaults, compaction: { model: 'gpt54mini' } } } }, provider: 'openai', modelId: 'gpt-5.5', authProfileId: 'openai:default', defaultProvider: 'openai', defaultModel: 'gpt-5.5' });
console.log('SAME_PROVIDER:', JSON.stringify({ provider: r1.provider, model: r1.model, authProfileId: r1.authProfileId }));
const r2 = resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget({ config: { ...cfg, agents: { defaults: { ...cfg.agents.defaults, compaction: { model: 'thinky' } } } }, provider: 'openai', modelId: 'gpt-5.5', authProfileId: 'openai:default', defaultProvider: 'openai', defaultModel: 'gpt-5.5' });
console.log('CROSS_PROVIDER:', JSON.stringify({ provider: r2.provider, model: r2.model, authProfileId: r2.authProfileId }));
const r3 = resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget({ config: { ...cfg, agents: { defaults: { ...cfg.agents.defaults, compaction: { model: 'nonexistent-alias' } } } }, provider: 'openai', modelId: 'gpt-5.5', authProfileId: 'openai:default', defaultProvider: 'openai', defaultModel: 'gpt-5.5' });
console.log('LITERAL_FALLBACK:', JSON.stringify({ provider: r3.provider, model: r3.model, authProfileId: r3.authProfileId }));
const r4 = resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget({ config: cfg, provider: 'openai', modelId: 'gpt-5.5', authProfileId: 'openai:default', defaultProvider: 'openai', defaultModel: 'gpt-5.5' });
console.log('NO_OVERRIDE:', JSON.stringify({ provider: r4.provider, model: r4.model, authProfileId: r4.authProfileId }));
const r5 = resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget({ config: { agents: { defaults: { models: { 'openai/gpt-5.4-mini': { alias: 'gpt54mini' }, 'openai/gpt54mini': {} }, compaction: { model: 'gpt54mini' } } } }, provider: 'openai', modelId: 'gpt-5.5', authProfileId: 'openai:default', defaultProvider: 'openai', defaultModel: 'gpt-5.5' });
console.log('COLLISION_LITERAL:', JSON.stringify({ provider: r5.provider, model: r5.model, authProfileId: r5.authProfileId }));
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
SAME_PROVIDER: {"provider":"openai","model":"gpt-5.4-mini","authProfileId":"openai:default"}
CROSS_PROVIDER: {"provider":"anthropic","model":"claude-opus-4-6"}
LITERAL_FALLBACK: {"provider":"openai","model":"nonexistent-alias","authProfileId":"openai:default"}
NO_OVERRIDE: {"provider":"openai","model":"gpt-5.5","authProfileId":"openai:default"}
COLLISION_LITERAL: {"provider":"openai","model":"gpt54mini","authProfileId":"openai:default"}
```

**Observed result after the fix:** The production `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget` correctly:
- Resolves `gpt54mini` alias to canonical `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` with auth profile preserved
- Resolves `thinky` alias to `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6` with auth profile dropped (provider change)
- Falls back to literal value when no alias matches (backward compatible)
- Preserves literal configured model ids when the same string is also an alias (`openai/gpt54mini` wins over alias `gpt54mini`)
- Passing no compaction model override uses the default provider/model unchanged

**What was not tested:** Live gateway auto-compaction with a real long-running session that triggers the embedded compaction path end-to-end.

## Tests and validation

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  22 passed (22)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  188 passed (188)

$ git diff --check
(no output)
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — bare compaction overrides that match configured aliases now resolve to canonical refs; literal configured model ids still win on alias collisions
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No (same config shape, interpretation aligns with shared model-selection helpers)
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area: alias vs literal precedence for existing compaction.model values
Mitigation: literal configured model id lookup runs before alias resolution; regression tests cover same-provider, cross-provider, unmatched alias, and collision precedence

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review
Waiting on: ClawSweeper re-review after shared-resolver + literal-precedence fix (head b133cb4ab9f)
Bot comments addressed:
- [P1] Replaced local alias scanner with `buildModelAliasIndex` + `resolveModelRefFromString`
- [P1] Preserved literal configured model ids before alias lookup via `inferUniqueProviderFromConfiguredModels`
- [P2] Added collision-precedence regression test
